channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: What a gastroenterologist wants you to know about bidets
item.description: Columnist Trisha Pasricha has received many questions from people who are bidet-curious but not yet bidet-ready.

item.title: Trump wanted to break the system. The system is breaking him instead.
item.description: The courts, Congress and the Constitution are doing what a weak opposition could not, Ramesh Ponnuru writes.

item.title: We visited the Trump-backed ‘Freedom Trucks’ crisscrossing the country. Here’s what we found.
item.description: At a time of fierce debate over how to teach American history, particularly around issues of race, the Freedom Trucks weigh in squarely on one side of the argument, telling a patriotic, positive story of core American values and exceptionalism. They stand in sharp contrast to liberal efforts in recent years to lift up discussion of systemic racism and highlight chapters where the United States has failed to live up to its ideals.

item.title: See where a heat dome may bring record-breaking temperatures this week
item.description: A dangerous heat dome is expected to intensify over the Eastern United States this week, pushing temperatures into the triple digits and probably breaking hundreds of records across three dozen states. The risk for heat-related impacts is forecast to reach major or extreme levels for more than 220 million Americans in total through Saturday, according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

item.title: Electric vehicle sales have collapsed, but hybrid vehicles are quietly taking over
item.description: While electric vehicles have been hit hard by shifting policies and drivers’ doubts, sales of hybrids are up 80 percent since 2023. Carmakers are taking notice.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Stepmom refuses to find more hobbies to foster a healthy retired life
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Stepmom refuses to find more hobbies to foster a healthy retired life

item.title: Medicare is about to cover GLP-1 drugs for weight loss. Here’s what to know.
item.description: Beginning Wednesday, the federal health insurance program will cover GLP-1 drugs for weight loss for the first time, with patients responsible for a $50-a-month co-payment that makes it far cheaper than cash prices without insurance. But there are important considerations under the Trump administration’s 18-month pilot.

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s military purges claim a superstar
item.description: Gen. Christopher Donahue heads into early retirement as the defense secretary seeks to root out MAGA disloyalty.

item.title: Referencing a president in today’s On the Record
item.description: Referencing a president in today’s On the Record

item.title: Supreme Court expands Trump’s power over the federal bureaucracy
item.description: The justices allowed the president to fire a Democratic member of the Federal Trade Commission, striking down a nearly century-old precedent that insulated independent agencies from political influence by the executive.

item.title: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Fed board member Lisa Cook
item.description: The decision is a setback for the president, who has pushed aggressively to exert greater control over parts of the federal government that Congress set up to be insulated from influence by the president. He tried to oust Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook, alleging she committed mortgage fraud.

item.title: Supreme Court rules mail-in ballots postmarked by Election Day that arrive later can be tallied
item.description: A decision against such ballots could have upended election procedures in states across the country ahead of the midterm elections.

item.title: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in E. Jean Carroll case
item.description: The high-profile 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting and defaming journalist E. Jean Carroll. The justices’ decision not to take up the case leaves in place a 2024 appeals court ruling that affirmed the judgment.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz. Midterm rules. Twain Prize. Heat dome. GLP-1 drugs. Ticks. Civics quiz. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: What Bucha taught me about Ukraine’s will to fight
item.description: What Bucha taught me about Ukraine’s will to fight

item.title: Why the nation’s founding document is marred by creases, stains and a mysterious handprint
item.description: The physical journey of the Declaration of Independence tells its own story. Its imperfections and endurance reflect those of the America it helped create.

item.title: A rare, and potentially fatal, tick-borne illness is spreading in the U.S.
item.description: Cases of Powassan virus have hit the news in recent months because of the potential severity of the symptoms.

item.title: With time running out, Trump digs in on changing midterm election rules amid avalanche of setbacks
item.description: Fearing a Democratic takeover of Congress, the president has spent months trying to alter how the midterms will be conducted, but obstacles keep cropping up.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Ignored by her friend group — until she mentions motherhood
item.description: Friends took no interest in letter writer’s life as the only non-mom in the group – till she said she was freezing her eggs.

item.title: Four Black women. Nine degrees. Not one steady paycheck.
item.description: The president promised to save “Black jobs,” but his policies have resulted in fresh pain for the Black middle class as the employment gap widens.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Play Washington Post's daily crossword puzzles online for free.

item.title: How Elbridge Colby became GOP lawmakers’ chief irritant in Trump’s Pentagon
item.description: The Defense Department undersecretary has become the central figure in a battle to define the future of “America First” foreign policy.

item.title: Rep. Julia Letlow of Louisiana wins Republican primary runoff in race to succeed Sen. Bill Cassidy, who lost after crossing Trump
item.description: Cassidy was one of seven Republican senators who voted to convict President Donald Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial. Letlow defeated John Fleming, the state treasurer, in Saturday’s runoff and is heavily favored in the general election in November in the deep-red state.

item.title: U.S. again strikes targets in Iran, as hostilities erupt over the vital Strait of Hormuz for a third day
item.description: The clashes marked the latest threat to a ceasefire and ongoing talks toward a broader peace. Central Command said the strikes were in response to what it called “continued Iranian aggression against commercial shipping.” Iran earlier said it was targeting U.S. interests in the Middle East.

item.title: How Poland’s fate drove the Declaration
item.description: How Poland’s fate drove the Declaration

item.title: Can you pass this quiz on American history and government?
item.description: As we celebrate the nation’s 250th birthday, test your civics knowledge and see how your score compares to others.

item.title: Trump taps former Oklahoma state trooper Lance Schroyer to lead ICE, agency at center of deportation campaign
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is nominating Schroyer to be the head of the agency driving his controversial effort to detain and deport millions of undocumented immigrants.

item.title: There’s an all-natural, chemical-free way to fix the Reflecting Pool, our columnist writes
item.description: Enlisting nature to help clean up the algae would be more patriotic than tossing in chemicals.

item.title: The Park Police allow chases. One just ended in a deliveryman’s death.
item.description: The Venezuelan immigrant was delivering food in D.C. when he was struck. Experts called the police chase reckless.

item.title: Unease deepens in Russia as Ukraine steps up long-range strikes
item.description: Experts said Vladimir Putin was unlikely to change course in his military campaign despite worsening fuel shortages and a sharp decline in the stock market.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Is husband right, missing kid’s college move-in is ‘unacceptable’?
item.description: Daughter would say it’s okay, but letter writer is unsure how to weigh the importance of a milestone against a rare career coup.

item.title: Trump reveals new images of passport with his portrait to mark America’s 250th
item.description: The State Department said the new document “boldly honors” the president and the nation's founding. The rendering differed from previous images of the passport in several ways.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: Trump’s Religious Liberty Commission takes aim at separation of church and state
item.description: The Trump administration issued a draft report from the commission that says the separation concept is a legal error and that Americans should view religion as an “essential support.” The 224-page report also recommended the DOJ issue guidance to promote “an originalist understanding” of how the Constitution sees the relationship between religion and government.

item.title: The Great American State Fair feels rushed, simulated and oddly sterile, our columnist Philip Kennicott writes
item.description: The Trump administration’s signature 250th celebration on the National Mall lacks nostalgia and feels more like a trade show for Christian groups, tourist boards and the military industrial complex.

item.title: Appeals court rules against Trump administration on air pollution limits
item.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit ruled that air pollution limits tightened by the Environmental Protection Agency under President Joe Biden should remain in force, thwarting efforts by the Trump administration, the U.S. Chamber of Commerce and other groups to vacate that standard and return to a previous one.

item.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military sites after ship was hit in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. targeted Iranian missile and drone storage locations Friday in what Central Command called a “powerful response” to “unwarranted aggression.” President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating a ceasefire that was solidified in a memorandum of understanding signed by the two countries last week.

item.title: The Supreme Court errs in this immigration ruling, George F. Will writes
item.description: The justices get it wrong on temporary protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants.

item.title: Ukrainian drones drive Russia to declare emergency in occupied Crimea
item.description: Weeks of punishing strikes have disrupted water, fuel and electricity supplies across the peninsula, which Ukraine’s president has vowed to reclaim.

item.title: Trump administration will decide who gets to use latest upgrade to ChatGPT, a major expansion of its regulation of Silicon Valley
item.description: The administration came to power preaching a laissez-faire approach to AI but has lately increased oversight of the industry.

item.title: Lewis George racks up most support in a D.C. mayoral primary in a generation, spanning race, class and geography
item.description: D.C.’s presumptive next mayor, a democratic socialist, has amassed more than 75,000 votes in the most dominant primary performance since Adrian Fenty won every precinct in 2006.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: My sister shut me out because of her religion. Do I attend her wedding?
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: My sister shut me out because of her religion. Do I attend her wedding?

item.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty in classified files case
item.description: Bolton pleaded guilty in federal court Friday. He served as national security adviser in Donald Trump’s first administration before becoming a vocal critic of the president.

item.title: I’m an evangelical. Here’s the real problem with the tradwife trend.
item.description: When it comes to marriage and motherhood, traditional and biblical are not the same.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.
item.description: Take today’s news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: Nursing homes, factories brace for fallout from Supreme Court ruling that could affect more than 1 million immigrants
item.description: The high court’s decision to strip Haitians and Syrians of their protections from deportation had immediate ripple effects for immigrants and their employers.

item.title: Supreme Court rulings. Iran attack. Venezuela rescuers. NYC rent freeze. RFK Jr. Europe heat deaths. Anti-vaxxers. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: California billionaire tax is headed for the ballot despite opposition from top Democrats
item.description: The push to put a billionaire tax to a popular vote in blue California has exposed deep divides on the left, even as Democratic politicians across the country rally around calls for the wealthy to pay more. The 5 percent tax would raise tens of billions for health care, but the issue is pitting the populist mood of the public against many Democrats’ fears that the measure will push the ultrawealthy to join a broader exodus from the state.

item.title: New York City passes rent freeze, delivering on a key piece of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s agenda
item.description: After months of hearings that included public and expert testimony, the Rent Guidelines Board handed down its decision, which caps rent for up to two years on about 40 percent of the New York’s housing stock — generally apartment buildings that were constructed before 1974. Landlords and real estate representatives panned the decision, saying it could devastate already-meager profits and drive bankruptcy.

item.title: What an expert in plant-based food eats for protein, calcium and more
item.description: We wanted to learn what a registered dietitian who follows a strictly plant-based diet eats in a typical day. So, we caught up with Nichole Dandrea-Russert to find out what her meals are like, why she avoids using the term “vegan” and why she recommends eating dark chocolate on a regular basis.
